stories.title: Prediction: This Artificial Intelligence (AI) Chip Stock Is the Biggest Winner From the $26.5 Billion SK Hynix IPO (Hint: It's Not Nvidia)
stories.simple_summary: A company that makes computer chips used in AI systems is expected to do well after another chip company had a big public stock sale.
stories.why_it_matters: This mostly matters to investors and people who own tech company stock — nothing you need to act on.

stories.title: Substack adds an AI detector to help spot blogs written by no one
stories.simple_summary: Substack, a platform where writers publish newsletters and blogs, is adding a tool that can detect whether text was written by AI or by a human.
stories.why_it_matters: If you read newsletters or blogs on Substack, you'll be able to check whether what you're reading was actually written by a person or created by AI.

stories.title: Data centers expected to use 4x more electricity by 2035
stories.simple_summary: The computers that power AI and store internet data will need four times as much electricity by 2035 as they use today.
stories.why_it_matters: This could eventually mean higher electricity costs overall, though it mostly reflects technical industry growth rather than something affecting you immediately.

stories.title: OpenAI backs watered-down Massachusetts AI safety bill
stories.simple_summary: OpenAI (the company behind ChatGPT) supported a Massachusetts law about AI safety, though the law was made less strict than originally planned.
stories.why_it_matters: This mostly matters to investors and policy experts — nothing you need to act on.

stories.title: Google releases three new Gemini models — but no 3.5 Pro
stories.simple_summary: Google released some new versions of its AI assistant Gemini, but didn't release an updated version many people were expecting.
stories.why_it_matters: If you use Google's AI tools, there are some new options available, but this is mainly of interest to tech enthusiasts.

stories.title: Anthropic’s $1.5 billion book piracy settlement approved by judge
stories.simple_summary: Anthropic, an AI company, has to pay $1.5 billion to authors whose copyrighted books were used to train its AI without permission, with authors getting around $3,000 per book.
stories.why_it_matters: This is good news for authors — it establishes that AI companies must pay creators when they use their work — though this mostly matters to writers rather than small business owners.

stories.title: Election voting advice from AI chatbots ‘inaccurate and unreliable’
stories.simple_summary: AI chatbots give voting advice that is often wrong or unreliable.
stories.why_it_matters: If you ask an AI chatbot for election information or voting guidance, you should check the facts yourself rather than trusting what it says.

stories.title: University of Tennessee sues Anthropic over neural network technology
stories.simple_summary: The University of Tennessee is suing Anthropic, an AI company, over a technology dispute.
stories.why_it_matters: This is a legal matter between organizations — nothing you need to act on.

stories.title: US threatens sanctions against Chinese AI models over IP theft
stories.simple_summary: The U.S. government is threatening to punish Chinese companies that make AI tools if they steal American technology.
stories.why_it_matters: This is mainly about government trade policy and international business — nothing affecting ordinary people or small businesses right now.

stories.title: Google launches a cheaper alternative to large AI security models like Mythos
stories.simple_summary: Google released a new, cheaper AI tool designed specifically to find and fix security problems in computer systems.
stories.why_it_matters: If your business uses security services, there's now a cheaper option available for protecting against computer attacks.

stories.title: Japan’s AI gamble: Can technology offset the cost of an ageing society?
stories.simple_summary: Japan is trying to use AI technology to help manage the costs of taking care of its older population.
stories.why_it_matters: This is mostly a news story about Japan's policy choices — nothing you need to act on.

stories.title: Music streamer Deezer says more than 50% of daily uploads are AI-generated
stories.simple_summary: On the music streaming service Deezer, more than half of all the songs uploaded every day are now made by AI instead of real musicians.
stories.why_it_matters: If you listen to music online, you're increasingly hearing AI-created music, which means less income for human musicians — and the quality and creativity of streamed music may change.
